sourceMovie.title: Trading Places
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Trading Places for the same reason it broke through in 1983: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a comedy rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. John Landis keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: The Wolf of Wall Street
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Trading Places clicked for you, The Wolf of Wall Street (2013) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Trading Places?
faq.answer: Try The Wolf of Wall Street, Back to the Future, Back to the Future Part II—each shares DNA with Trading Places while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Trading Places?
faq.answer: Trading Places (1983) is best described as Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Trading Places worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
